human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for restaurant menu and pricing boundary discipline, source quality, refund and pricing-policy boundaries, guest-safety and fraud escalation, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to restaurant menu and pricing execution, channel publication, order and payment mismatch review, completion evidence, and exception routing.
Authority sources cover FDA menu-labeling and Food Code references, FTC restaurant delivery pricing and blocked-funds enforcement, and PCI SSC payment-security standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete inputs, missing completion evidence, payment mismatches, high-volume rollout, refund-boundary rejection, guest-safety escalation, fraud escalation, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-service-and-restaurants/menu-and-pricing-operations-specialist/ so restaurant menu and pricing work is separated from adjacent guest-services, pricing or revenue, fraud, and food-safety lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around pricing approval, refund overrides, guest-safety handling, and fraud or loss-prevention routing.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated restaurant menu and pricing operations specialist that supports evidence-backed publication handling, order and payment exception review, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
